description: PM-KISAN is a Central Sector scheme with 100% funding from Government of India. Under the scheme, income support of Rs. 6,000 per year is provided to all farmer families across the country in three equal installments of Rs. 2,000 each every four months. The fund is directly transferred to the bank accounts of the beneficiaries.
eligibility: All landholding farmer families are eligible, subject to certain exclusion criteria. The farmer must be a citizen of India. Institutional landholders, farmer families holding constitutional posts, serving or retired officers and employees of state/central government, income tax payers, and professionals like doctors, engineers, lawyers, and chartered accountants are excluded.
benefits: Financial benefit of Rs. 6,000 per year in three installments of Rs. 2,000 each. Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to bank account. Covers all landholding farmer families. Provides income support for purchasing inputs related to agriculture.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account with IFSC code, Land ownership documents or land records, State/UT specific domicile certificate if required.
application_process: Step 1: Visit the official PM-KISAN portal at pmkisan.gov.in. Step 2: Click on 'New Farmer Registration'. Step 3: Select Rural or Urban farmer type. Step 4: Enter Aadhaar number, mobile number, and select state. Step 5: Fill in personal details, bank account details, and land information. Step 6: Submit the registration form. Step 7: Application will be verified by state nodal officers. Step 8: Upon approval, benefits will be credited directly to the bank account.

description: PMAY is a flagship mission of the Government of India which addresses urban and rural housing shortage. It aims to provide affordable housing to the urban poor and rural households by 2024. The scheme provides financial assistance for construction of pucca houses with basic amenities including toilet, electricity, LPG connection, and water supply.
eligibility: Beneficiary family should not own a pucca house anywhere in India. For PMAY-Urban: Annual household income up to Rs. 3,00,000 for EWS category, up to Rs. 6,00,000 for LIG category, up to Rs. 12,00,000 for MIG-I, and up to Rs. 18,00,000 for MIG-II. For PMAY-Gramin: Beneficiary must be from BPL category, SC/ST, freed bonded labourers, minorities, or manual scavengers. Women ownership or co-ownership is mandatory for EWS and LIG categories.
benefits: Credit-linked subsidy of Rs. 2,67,280 for EWS/LIG (interest subsidy of 6.5% on loan up to Rs. 6 lakhs for 20 years). Subsidy of Rs. 2,35,068 for MIG-I (4% on loan up to Rs. 9 lakhs). Subsidy of Rs. 2,30,156 for MIG-II (3% on loan up to Rs. 12 lakhs). Under PMAY-Gramin, assistance of Rs. 1,20,000 in plain areas and Rs. 1,30,000 in hilly areas.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card of all family members, Proof of Income (salary slip, ITR, or income certificate), Proof of property or land documents, Bank account details, Caste certificate (if applicable), Affidavit stating no pucca house in India, Passport-size photographs.
application_process: Step 1: Visit pmaymis.gov.in (Urban) or pmayg.nic.in (Rural). Step 2: Click on 'Citizen Assessment' under 'Benefits under other 3 components'. Step 3: Enter Aadhaar number for verification. Step 4: Fill in personal, family, income, bank, and property details. Step 5: Upload required documents. Step 6: Submit the application. Step 7: Application is verified by Urban Local Body/Gram Panchayat. Step 8: Upon approval, subsidy is credited through bank.
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

description: The Kisan Credit Card scheme provides adequate and timely credit support from the banking system under a single window to farmers for their cultivation and other needs including purchase of agricultural inputs, draw cash for production needs, and post-harvest expenses. The scheme also covers term loans for agriculture and allied activities plus a reasonable component for consumption needs of farmer households.
eligibility: All farmers — individual/joint borrowers who are owner cultivators, tenant farmers, oral lessees, and sharecroppers. Self Help Groups (SHGs) or Joint Liability Groups (JLGs) of farmers including tenant farmers and sharecroppers. Fishermen and animal husbandry farmers are also eligible under KCC. Must be at least 18 years and not more than 75 years of age.
benefits: Flexible credit limit based on crop cultivated and area. Interest rate of 7% p.a. with 3% interest subvention for prompt repayment (effective 4%). Crop insurance coverage under PMFBY at nominal premium. Personal accident insurance cover of Rs. 50,000 for death and Rs. 25,000 for disability. No collateral required for loans up to Rs. 1.6 lakhs.
documents_required: Duly filled application form, Identity proof (Aadhaar Card / Voter ID / PAN Card), Address proof, Land records (7/12 extract, Record of Rights), Passport-size photographs, Bank account details, Crop sowing certificate from Patwari.
application_process: Step 1: Visit your nearest bank branch (any scheduled commercial bank, cooperative bank, or RRB). Step 2: Collect the KCC application form. Step 3: Fill in personal, land, and crop details. Step 4: Attach required identity and land documents. Step 5: Submit the completed form to the bank. Step 6: Bank officials conduct field verification. Step 7: Upon approval, KCC card and cheque book are issued. Step 8: Credit limit is sanctioned based on scale of finance and land holding.

description: MUDRA Yojana provides loans up to Rs. 10 lakhs to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises engaged in manufacturing, trading, service sector, and activities allied to agriculture. Loans are provided under three categories: Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishore (Rs. 50,001 to Rs. 5 lakhs), and Tarun (Rs. 5,00,001 to Rs. 10 lakhs). These loans are given by Commercial Banks, RRBs, Small Finance Banks, MFIs, and NBFCs.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen who has a business plan for non-farm income generating activity such as manufacturing, processing, trading, or service sector. The applicant should not be a defaulter to any bank/financial institution. Business enterprise should be a non-corporate, non-farm sector enterprise. Applicable for proprietorship, partnership firms, and individuals.
benefits: Loans up to Rs. 10 lakhs without collateral. No processing charges under Shishu category. Flexible repayment period of up to 5 years. MUDRA Card for working capital requirements. Lower interest rates compared to informal sources of lending.
documents_required: Identity proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, PAN), Address proof (utility bills, Aadhaar), Business address proof, Last 6 months bank statements, Business plan or project report, Proof of business existence (registration, licenses), Passport-size photographs, Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC if applicable).
application_process: Step 1: Visit any bank, NBFC, or MFI offering MUDRA loans. Step 2: Identify the appropriate category (Shishu/Kishore/Tarun) based on loan requirement. Step 3: Collect and fill the MUDRA loan application form. Step 4: Prepare a brief business plan or project report. Step 5: Attach required identity, address, and business documents. Step 6: Submit the application. Step 7: Bank conducts due diligence and credit appraisal. Step 8: Upon approval, loan is disbursed. Also available online via mudra.org.in or bank websites.

description: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao is a nation-wide campaign of the Government of India that aims to generate awareness and improve the efficiency of welfare services intended for girls. The scheme addresses the declining Child Sex Ratio (CSR) and related issues of women empowerment. It is a joint initiative of the Ministry of Women and Child Development, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, and Ministry of Education.
eligibility: All families having girl children from birth to 10 years of age. The Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana under BBBP is for girl children below 10 years. Indian citizens from all states and union territories are eligible. No income restriction applies.
benefits: Under Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana, investment with interest rate of 8.2% per annum (as of 2024). Tax benefits under Section 80C of Income Tax Act. Maturity amount can be used for higher education and marriage of the girl child. Awareness programs for girl education. Prevention of gender-biased sex selection. Ensuring survival and protection of the girl child.
documents_required: Birth certificate of the girl child, Aadhaar Card of guardian/parent, Address proof, Passport-size photographs of guardian and child, Medical records (birth hospital details).
application_process: Step 1: Visit any authorized bank or post office. Step 2: Open a Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) account using the prescribed form. Step 3: Provide birth certificate and parent's identity proof. Step 4: Make the initial deposit (minimum Rs. 250 per year). Step 5: Continue deposits for 15 years. Step 6: Partial withdrawal allowed after girl turns 18. Step 7: Account matures after 21 years from opening.
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development

description: Startup India is a flagship initiative of the Government of India intended to catalyze startup culture and build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation and startups in the country. The Action Plan comprises of 19 action items spanning across areas such as simplification and handholding, funding support, and industry-academia partnership.
eligibility: Entity should be incorporated as a Private Limited Company, Registered Partnership, or Limited Liability Partnership. Entity age should not exceed 10 years from the date of incorporation. Turnover should not exceed Rs. 100 crore in any financial year since incorporation. Entity must be working towards innovation, development, or improvement of products/services/processes with scalable business model and high potential for employment generation or wealth creation.
benefits: Self-certification-based compliance for 9 labour and 3 environment laws. Income tax exemption for 3 consecutive years out of 10 years since incorporation. Exemption from angel tax under Section 56(2)(viib). Fund of Funds with corpus of Rs. 10,000 crore managed by SIDBI. Fast-tracked patent application at rebated fees. Easy winding up of company within 90 days under Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code.
documents_required: Certificate of Incorporation, PAN of the entity, Details of Directors/Partners, Brief about the business and innovation, Proof of concept or prototype, Financial statements, Udyam Registration (optional but recommended).
application_process: Step 1: Incorporate your business as Private Limited, LLP, or Partnership. Step 2: Register on Startup India portal at startupindia.gov.in. Step 3: Fill in the startup recognition application. Step 4: Upload required documents including a brief about innovation. Step 5: Submit application for DPIIT recognition. Step 6: Receive DPIIT recognition number. Step 7: Apply for tax benefits and funding through the portal.
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: PMJJBY is a government-backed life insurance scheme offering life coverage of Rs. 2 lakhs for death due to any reason. It is a one-year cover renewable term life insurance plan. The premium is Rs. 436 per annum (as of 2023) which is auto-debited from the subscriber's bank account. It is available to people in the age group of 18 to 50 years having a bank account.
eligibility: Any individual between 18 to 50 years of age with a savings bank account. Single bank account holder can join. Aadhaar is the primary KYC for the bank account. The coverage period is from 1st June to 31st May. Must give consent for auto-debit of premium amount.
benefits: Life insurance cover of Rs. 2,00,000 on death of the insured due to any reason. Premium of only Rs. 436 per year. Available to all bank account holders. No medical examination required. Simple enrollment through banks.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Savings bank account, Consent for auto-debit, Nominee details, Aadhaar linkage with bank account.
application_process: Step 1: Ensure you have a savings bank account with Aadhaar linked. Step 2: Visit your bank or use net banking/mobile banking. Step 3: Fill the consent-cum-declaration form for PMJJBY. Step 4: Provide nominee details. Step 5: Give auto-debit consent for yearly premium. Step 6: Premium of Rs. 436 is auto-debited annually on or before 31st May.

description: PMSBY is an accident insurance scheme offering coverage for death or disability due to accident at a premium of Rs. 20 per annum. It covers accidental death (Rs. 2 lakhs), total permanent disability (Rs. 2 lakhs), and partial permanent disability (Rs. 1 lakh). The scheme is available for people aged 18 to 70 years with a bank account.
eligibility: Any individual between 18 to 70 years of age having a savings bank account. Aadhaar is the primary KYC. The coverage is from 1st June to 31st May each year. One policy per person. Consent for auto-debit of Rs. 20 premium.
benefits: Accidental death cover of Rs. 2,00,000. Total permanent disability cover of Rs. 2,00,000. Partial permanent disability cover of Rs. 1,00,000. Ultra-low premium of Rs. 20 per year. Simple enrollment through bank. No medical examination.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Savings bank account linked with Aadhaar, Consent form for auto-debit, Nominee details.
application_process: Step 1: Ensure you have a savings bank account. Step 2: Visit the bank or use net/mobile banking. Step 3: Fill the PMSBY application form. Step 4: Provide nominee details. Step 5: Give consent for auto-debit. Step 6: Premium of Rs. 20 debited from account annually.

description: APY is a government-backed pension scheme targeted at workers in the unorganised sector. It guarantees a minimum monthly pension of Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000 after the age of 60 depending on the contribution. The central government co-contributes 50% of the total contribution or Rs. 1,000 per annum, whichever is lower, for 5 years for subscribers who joined the scheme before March 2016.
eligibility: Citizen of India between 18 to 40 years of age. Must have a savings bank account. Not a member of any statutory social security scheme. Not an income tax payer. Aadhaar and mobile number linked to bank account.
benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension of Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000 after age 60. Government co-contribution for eligible subscribers. Spouse continues to receive pension after subscriber's death. Full pension corpus returned to nominee after death of both subscriber and spouse. Tax benefit under Section 80CCD.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank account with Aadhaar and mobile number linked, Age proof if not in Aadhaar.
application_process: Step 1: Visit your bank where you have a savings account. Step 2: Fill the APY registration form. Step 3: Choose pension amount (Rs. 1,000/2,000/3,000/4,000/5,000 per month). Step 4: Provide Aadhaar and mobile number. Step 5: Provide nominee details. Step 6: Monthly/quarterly/half-yearly auto-debit starts from bank account. Also available via net banking of most banks.

description: PMJDY is the National Mission for Financial Inclusion to ensure access to financial services, namely, banking/savings and deposit accounts, remittance, credit, insurance, and pension in an affordable manner. Under the scheme, a basic savings bank account can be opened in any bank by those who do not have any bank account.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen above the age of 10 years can open a bank account under this scheme. No minimum balance is required. Minor accounts (10-18 years) can be managed by guardians. One account per household. Special focus on unbanked households.
benefits: Zero balance bank account. Free RuPay debit card. Accidental insurance cover of Rs. 2 lakhs (with RuPay card). Life insurance cover of Rs. 30,000 for accounts opened between 15 Aug 2014 to 31 Jan 2015. Overdraft facility of Rs. 10,000 for one account per household. Access to Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). Easy access to credit and insurance.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card (Aadhaar alone is sufficient), or Voter ID/Driving License/PAN/Passport with self-declaration of address. Passport-size photograph.
application_process: Step 1: Visit any bank branch or Bank Mitra (Business Correspondent). Step 2: Fill in the account opening form. Step 3: Submit Aadhaar or other identity proof. Step 4: Provide passport-size photograph. Step 5: Account is opened with zero balance. Step 6: Receive RuPay debit card. Step 7: Start using banking services.

description: PMFBY provides comprehensive insurance coverage against failure of crop due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases. Farmers pay only 2% premium for Kharif crops, 1.5% for Rabi crops, and 5% for commercial/horticultural crops. The remaining premium is shared by central and state governments on 50:50 basis. Claims are settled using advanced technology including satellite imagery and drones.
eligibility: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops in notified areas. Both loanee and non-loanee farmers are eligible. Mandatory for loanee farmers and voluntary for non-loanee farmers. Available in all states that have signed MoU.
benefits: Crop insurance at subsidized premium (2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for commercial crops). Coverage against all non-preventable natural risks from pre-sowing to post-harvest. Individual farm-level assessment for localized calamities. Use of modern technology for quick claim settlement. Add-on coverage for loss due to wild animals and other perils.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank passbook, Land records (7/12 extract or Khasra/Khatauni), Sowing certificate, Crop details and area, Bank loan account details (for loanee farmers).
application_process: Step 1: Visit the PMFBY portal at pmfby.gov.in or your nearest bank/CSC centre. Step 2: Select the season, year, state, district, and crop. Step 3: Fill in land and crop sowing details. Step 4: Calculate premium using the online calculator. Step 5: Pay the farmer share of premium. Step 6: Receive policy confirmation. Step 7: In case of crop loss, report within 72 hours through the Crop Insurance App or call centre.

description: PMKVY is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship for skill development of youth. It aims to enable Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training that helps them in securing a better livelihood. Training is provided free of cost and includes short-term training, recognition of prior learning, and special projects.
eligibility: Indian nationals who are school/college dropouts or unemployed youth. Any candidate with 10th/12th pass or graduate qualification. For Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL): Prior learning experience or skills acquired informally. Age: 15 to 45 years. No income criterion.
benefits: Free skill training in industry-relevant courses. Training duration: 150-300 hours depending on the job role. Monetary reward of Rs. 8,000 on average upon successful certification. Placement assistance post-training. NSQF-aligned certification recognized across industries. On-the-job training component. Mentoring and support after placement.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Educational certificates (10th/12th marksheet), Bank account details, Passport-size photographs, Residence proof.
application_process: Step 1: Visit the PMKVY portal at pmkvyofficial.org. Step 2: Find a training centre near you. Step 3: Select a skill course aligned to your interest. Step 4: Enroll at the training centre with required documents. Step 5: Complete the training program. Step 6: Appear for assessment by SSC-assigned assessment body. Step 7: Receive certification and monetary reward upon successful assessment. Step 8: Avail placement support.
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

scheme_name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
description: DAY-NRLM aims to reduce poverty by enabling poor households to access gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment opportunities. It mobilizes rural poor women into Self Help Groups (SHGs) and provides them with long-term support through capacity building, financial inclusion, livelihood enhancement, and social development.
eligibility: Rural poor women from Below Poverty Line (BPL) families. At least one woman member from each identified poor household. Priority to vulnerable communities including SC/ST, minorities, and persons with disabilities. Women-focused — only women can be SHG members.
benefits: Revolving Fund of Rs. 10,000-15,000 per SHG. Community Investment Fund (CIF) up to Rs. 2.5 lakhs per SHG. Interest subvention on bank loans (3-4% for loans up to Rs. 3 lakhs). Skills training and capacity building. Digital financial literacy. Market linkage support.
documents_required: BPL certificate / Antyodaya Anna Yojana card, Aadhaar Card, Bank account details, Proof of residence, Caste certificate if applicable.
application_process: Step 1: Contact your village Panchayat or Block Development Office. Step 2: Get identified as part of Participatory Identification of Poor (PIP). Step 3: Form or join a Self Help Group (10-20 women per SHG). Step 4: Register the SHG at the block level. Step 5: Open a bank account for the SHG. Step 6: Start regular savings and internal lending within the group. Step 7: Apply for revolving fund and bank credit linkage through Community Resource Persons.

description: The National Scholarship Portal is a one-stop platform for various central and state government scholarships. It includes Pre-Matric Scholarship for Minorities, Post-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC students, Central Sector Scheme for College and University Students, and many state-specific scholarships. NSP provides a simplified process for applying and tracking scholarship applications.
eligibility: Students studying in recognized institutions from Class 1 to PhD level. Income criteria varies: Pre-Matric (family income up to Rs. 1 lakh p.a.), Post-Matric for SC/ST/OBC (income up to Rs. 2-2.5 lakhs p.a.), Central Sector Scheme (family income up to Rs. 8 lakhs p.a., must be in top 20th percentile in qualifying exam). Must not be receiving any other scholarship.
benefits: Pre-Matric: Up to Rs. 10,000 per annum for maintenance and fees. Post-Matric SC/ST: Full tuition fees and maintenance allowance. Post-Matric OBC: Tuition and examination fees. Central Sector Scheme: Rs. 10,000 to Rs. 20,000 per annum at graduation/post-graduation level. Top Class Education Scheme for SC/ST: Full fees at premier institutions.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Previous year marksheets, Income certificate of parents/guardian, Domicile certificate, Caste certificate (for SC/ST/OBC categories), Bank account details linked with Aadhaar, Bonafide certificate from institution, Fee receipt, Passport-size photograph.
application_process: Step 1: Visit the NSP portal at scholarships.gov.in. Step 2: Register using Aadhaar or other ID. Step 3: Login and select the applicable scholarship scheme. Step 4: Fill the application form with personal, academic, and bank details. Step 5: Upload scanned documents. Step 6: Get the application verified by your institution. Step 7: Institute forwards to the state/district nodal officer. Step 8: After verification, scholarship amount is disbursed to bank account via DBT.

description: PMUY aims to safeguard the health of women and children by providing them with clean cooking fuel – LPG. Under the scheme, free LPG connections are provided to women from BPL households. The government provides a subsidy of Rs. 1,600 per connection. The scheme empowers women and protects their health from the hazards of cooking with traditional fuels.
eligibility: Women belonging to BPL families, not having an LPG connection already. Priority categories: SC/ST households, Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY-G) beneficiaries, Antyodaya Anna Yojana beneficiaries, forest dwellers, most backward classes, tea and ex-tea garden tribes, people residing in islands. Age: Must be 18 years or above.
benefits: Free LPG connection with subsidy of Rs. 1,600. Free first LPG refill and stove in some states. EMI facility for purchase of stove and first refill. Improved health for women and children. Reduction in indoor air pollution. Time savings from not using traditional fuels.
documents_required: BPL certificate or Ration Card, Aadhaar Card, Jan Dhan bank account or any nationalized bank account, Passport-size photograph, A 14-point declaration form.
application_process: Step 1: Visit the nearest LPG distributor (Indane, HP Gas, Bharat Gas). Step 2: Collect the PMUY application form or download from pmuy.gov.in. Step 3: Fill in the KYC form with family details. Step 4: Submit BPL certificate, Aadhaar, bank details. Step 5: Distributor verifies eligibility through SECC data. Step 6: Upon approval, LPG connection is issued. Step 7: Receive subsidy directly in bank account for subsequent refills.
ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
description: AB-PMJAY is the world's largest health assurance scheme fully financed by the government. It provides health cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to over 12 crore poor and vulnerable families. The scheme covers 3 days of pre-hospitalization and 15 days of post-hospitalization expenses including diagnostics and medicines.
eligibility: Families identified based on deprivation and occupational criteria of SECC 2011 data. Rural: Families with no adult member aged 16-59, female-headed households, disabled member, SC/ST, manual casual laborers, landless, destitute, beggars, etc. Urban: Rag pickers, beggars, domestic workers, street vendors, construction workers, plumbers, painters, sanitation workers, etc. No restriction on family size, age, or gender.
benefits: Health insurance cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year. Cashless and paperless treatment at empanelled hospitals. Coverage for 1,929 medical procedures including surgery, medical and day care treatments. Pre-existing diseases covered from day one. No cap on family size. Both public and empanelled private hospitals covered.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or Ration Card, SECC 2011 database identification, Any government-issued photo ID, Family details. Golden Card (Ayushman Card) is generated after verification.
application_process: Step 1: Check eligibility at mera.pmjay.gov.in by entering mobile number or ration card number. Step 2: Visit the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) or empanelled hospital. Step 3: Carry Aadhaar card and ration card for verification. Step 4: Biometric authentication at CSC. Step 5: Ayushman Card (Golden Card) is generated. Step 6: Use the card at any empanelled hospital for cashless treatment. Step 7: Hospital bills are settled directly by the insurance company.
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
description: MGNREGA guarantees 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work. The wages are paid within 15 days of work. If employment is not provided within 15 days, an unemployment allowance is payable. The scheme also creates productive assets and strengthens natural resource management.
eligibility: Any adult member of a rural household willing to do unskilled manual work. Must be a resident of the Gram Panchayat they are applying in. Age: 18 years and above. No income limit. Each household gets a Job Card. Both male and female members are eligible, with one-third of beneficiaries being women.
benefits: Guaranteed 100 days of wage employment per household per year. Wages as per state-notified rates (Rs. 200-350 per day based on state). Unemployment allowance if work not provided within 15 days. Work within 5 km radius of village. Equal wages for men and women. Worksite facilities including crèche, drinking water, shade.
documents_required: Application for registration to Gram Panchayat, Photograph of adult family members, Age proof, Address proof, Bank/post office account details.
application_process: Step 1: Apply for a Job Card at your Gram Panchayat. Step 2: Gram Panchayat issues Job Card within 15 days after verification. Step 3: Submit a written application for work to the Gram Panchayat. Step 4: Receive dated receipt of application. Step 5: Work is to be provided within 15 days of application. Step 6: Attendance is marked at worksite through MIS. Step 7: Wages are credited to bank/post office account within 15 days.

description: Stand-Up India facilitates bank loans between Rs. 10 lakhs and Rs. 1 crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch. The scheme is for greenfield enterprises in manufacturing, services, trading sector, or agri-allied activities. It provides handholding support including startup training and credit guarantee.
eligibility: SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs aged 18 and above. For non-individual enterprises, at least 51% shareholding and controlling stake should be held by SC/ST or woman entrepreneur. The borrower should not be in default to any bank/financial institution. Greenfield (new) enterprises only. Existing businesses are not eligible.
benefits: Composite loan of Rs. 10 lakhs to Rs. 1 crore covering term loan and working capital. Repayment period up to 7 years. Moratorium period of up to 18 months. Credit guarantee coverage under CGFSIL. Handholding and mentorship support. Online application through standupmitra.in portal.
documents_required: Identity proof (Aadhaar/PAN), Address proof, Caste certificate (for SC/ST), Business project report, Proof of business premises, IT returns of last 2 years (if any), Bank statements, Quotations for machinery/equipment.
application_process: Step 1: Visit standupmitra.in portal. Step 2: Register as an applicant. Step 3: Connect with the nearest bank branch through the portal. Step 4: Prepare a detailed project report with SIDBI's support. Step 5: Submit loan application with required documents to the bank. Step 6: Bank processes the application. Step 7: Loan is sanctioned and disbursed. Step 8: Avail handholding support for business setup.

scheme_name: SWAYAM (Study Webs of Active-learning for Young Aspiring Minds)
description: SWAYAM is an online learning platform offering free courses from Class 9 to post-graduation level. Anyone from any background can access these courses. The platform hosts courses taught by the best teachers in India from IITs, IIMs, and central universities. SWAYAM courses are free and provide certificates for a nominal fee. Credits earned can be transferred to the academic record.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen or learner can access courses on SWAYAM. For credit transfer: Students enrolled in recognized universities/colleges. No age restriction. No income criterion. Free to all. For SWAYAM-NPTEL courses: Engineering students and professionals.
benefits: Free access to courses from top institutions. Video lectures, reading material, self-assessment tests, and online discussion forum. Certificate upon completing the course with fee of Rs. 100-1,000. Credit transfer facility recognized by UGC. Wide range of courses from humanities to engineering. Learn at your own pace.
documents_required: Online registration only. Email address. College enrollment details (for credit transfer). Aadhaar or ID for certificate verification.
application_process: Step 1: Visit swayam.gov.in. Step 2: Register with email address. Step 3: Browse available courses by subject, level, or institution. Step 4: Enroll in desired courses (no limit on number). Step 5: Follow the course schedule for video lectures and assignments. Step 6: Complete assessments and appear for certification exam (optional). Step 7: Download certificate. Step 8: Apply for credit transfer through your institution.

description: PMEGP is a credit-linked subsidy programme aimed at generating self-employment opportunities through establishment of micro-enterprises in non-farm sector. It is administered by the Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC). The maximum project cost is Rs. 50 lakhs for manufacturing and Rs. 20 lakhs for service sector.
eligibility: Any individual above 18 years of age. Minimum education: 8th pass for projects above Rs. 10 lakhs in manufacturing and above Rs. 5 lakhs in service. Self Help Groups, institutions registered under Societies Registration Act, production cooperative societies, and charitable trusts are also eligible. Only new projects are eligible. Existing units or units that have availed government subsidy are not eligible.
benefits: Subsidy of 15% to 35% of the project cost depending on category and location. General category: 15% (urban) to 25% (rural). Special category (SC/ST/OBC/Women/Minorities/Ex-servicemen/Disabled): 25% (urban) to 35% (rural). Maximum project cost of Rs. 50 lakhs (manufacturing) and Rs. 20 lakhs (service). Margin money (own contribution): 10% for general and 5% for special category.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Project report, Educational certificates (8th pass or above), Caste/category certificate (if applicable), EDP training certificate, Special category certificate (if applicable), Identity and address proof.
application_process: Step 1: Visit the PMEGP portal at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal. Step 2: Register and fill the online application form. Step 3: Select the type of activity (manufacturing/service). Step 4: Upload project report and required documents. Step 5: Application is screened by KVIC/KVIB/DIC at district level. Step 6: Bank sanctions the loan. Step 7: Complete mandatory EDP training. Step 8: Subsidy (margin money) is released to the bank by KVIC after bank disburses the loan.
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises

description: PMMVY is a maternity benefit programme providing cash incentive of Rs. 5,000 in three installments to pregnant women and lactating mothers for the first living child. The scheme aims to provide partial compensation for wage loss during pregnancy, safe delivery, and improved nutrition. For the second child (if girl), additional Rs. 6,000 is provided.
eligibility: Pregnant women and lactating mothers for the first living child. For second child benefit, the child must be a girl. Must be 19 years of age or above. Not applicable to government employees or those receiving similar benefits from other schemes. Must register pregnancy at Anganwadi Centre or approved health facility.
benefits: Cash incentive of Rs. 5,000 in three installments: Rs. 1,000 after registration of pregnancy, Rs. 2,000 after 6 months of pregnancy (at least one ANC), Rs. 2,000 after child birth registration and first cycle of immunization. Additional Rs. 6,000 for second child if girl. Directly transferred to bank/post office account.
documents_required: Mother and Child Protection (MCP) Card, Aadhaar Card of beneficiary, Bank/post office account details linked with Aadhaar, Registration of pregnancy at Anganwadi/health facility, Birth certificate of child, Immunization record.
application_process: Step 1: Register pregnancy at the nearest Anganwadi Centre (AWC) or health facility. Step 2: Fill Form 1-A for the first installment with pregnancy registration details. Step 3: Fill Form 1-B for the second installment after completing 6 months of pregnancy. Step 4: Fill Form 1-C for the third installment after child birth and first immunization. Step 5: Submit at AWC with required documents. Step 6: Benefits credited to bank account after verification at each stage.
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development

description: SBM-G aims to achieve Open Defecation Free (ODF) status and improve solid and liquid waste management in rural areas. Under the scheme, financial assistance of Rs. 12,000 is provided per household for construction of Individual Household Latrines (IHHL). The scheme now focuses on ODF sustainability and solid/liquid waste management (grey water, plastic waste, biodegradable waste).
eligibility: Rural households that do not have a toilet. Priority to BPL families, SC/ST households, physically handicapped, women-headed households, small and marginal farmers. All rural areas across India. No income restriction for construction of toilets under ODF Plus.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 12,000 per household for toilet construction. Additional incentive for converting insanitary to sanitary latrines. Support for solid and liquid waste management at village level. Community Sanitary Complexes. Behaviour change communication and IEC activities. Subsidy for setting up waste management infrastructure.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, BPL Card or Ration Card, Bank account details, Photograph of household (before and after construction), Application form filled at Gram Panchayat.
application_process: Step 1: Contact your Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office. Step 2: Submit application for toilet construction under SBM-G. Step 3: Gram Panchayat verifies eligibility. Step 4: Get approval and start construction of toilet. Step 5: Submit photographs of completed toilet. Step 6: Gram Panchayat verifies construction. Step 7: Financial assistance of Rs. 12,000 is credited to bank account in installments. Also available through SBM app.

description: PMGSY aims to provide all-weather road connectivity to eligible unconnected habitations in rural areas. Under PMGSY-III launched in 2019, the focus is on consolidation of through routes and major rural links connecting habitations to gramin agricultural markets, higher secondary schools, and hospitals. The scheme covers road construction and upgradation in rural India.
eligibility: Rural habitations with population of 500+ in plain areas and 250+ in hill states, tribal, and desert areas that are unconnected by all-weather roads. State governments propose habitations based on core network plans. This is a community-level scheme - individual citizens benefit through improved connectivity.
benefits: All-weather road connectivity for rural habitations. Upgradation of existing routes to higher specifications. Rural bridge construction. Employment generation during construction (under convergence with MGNREGA). Better access to markets, schools, and hospitals. Improved rural economy and quality of life.
documents_required: This is a government infrastructure scheme. Individual citizens do not apply directly. Village Panchayats and district administrations coordinate. Citizens can request road connectivity through Gram Sabha or write to the District Rural Development Agency (DRDA).
application_process: Step 1: This is a government-implemented scheme - individual applications are not required. Step 2: Village connectivity needs are identified through Core Network plans. Step 3: District/State level committees prioritize habitations. Step 4: DPRs (Detailed Project Reports) are prepared by state agencies. Step 5: Central government approves proposals. Step 6: Construction begins through contracted agencies. Step 7: Citizens can track road construction progress at pmgsy.nic.in.
